rationale: A cmd.exe process running as corp\Administrator at High integrity level orchestrated four sequential curl.exe invocations (A0, EV-002, EV-004, EV-006) using the -k flag (TLS certificate validation bypass) to download a 64-bit DLL from a raw GitHub commit URL into multiple staging locations: C:\Users\Public\Music\ (EV-001, EV-003), C:\ProgramData\ (EV-005), and the user's Temp directory (EV-007). The redundant multi-destination download pattern strongly indicates a payload staging/persistence attempt designed to survive partial cleanup, and dropping a DLL into world-writable paths (Public\Music, ProgramData, Temp) is a classic precursor to DLL hijacking or reflective loading. The use of -k to suppress certificate errors and the raw GitHub commit pinning (specific SHA) are consistent with attacker-controlled payload delivery infrastructure.
